---
title: "ALS loop"
author: "Jirka Vomlel"
date: "2026-04-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(RColorBrewer)
library(rTensor)
source("ALS.R")
# define nine colors 
col <- brewer.pal(n=9, name="Set1")
# remove yellow color, which is not well visible
col <- col[-6]
```

Read data from file "data.csv"

```{r}
d <- read.csv(file="data.csv")
```

Probability tables we will do experiments with

```{r}
vars.list <- list(
  c("d11","a15","a18","a19"),
  c("d1","d3","d6","d9","d13"),
  c("a17","a20","a28"),
  c("a1","a4"),
  c("d5","d11"),
  c("a2","a4","a18"),
  c("a1","a7","a8","a11"),
  c("a2","a3","a4","a9","a18"),
  c("d2","d7","d13"),
  c("d14","d5"),
  c("a4","a9","a10","a24"),
  c("a7","a11","a15","a19","a26"),
  c("a14","a22","a25"),
  c("a6","a13"),
  c("d4","a16"),
  c("a18","a28","a30"),
  c("d10","d8","a17","a28"),
  c("a1","a19","a27","d11","a18"),
  c("a6","a8","a20"),
  c("a1","a19")
)
```

Initialize the vectors for storing ranks, dimensions and errors for both the general and unimodal algoriths

```{r}
rnks <- 2:7
nr.distr <- length(vars.list)*length(rnks)
rank.dim.error <- array(0,dim = c(nr.distr,4))
colnames(rank.dim.error) <- c("index", "rank","dim","error")
general.rank.dim.error <- array(0,dim = c(nr.distr,4))
colnames(general.rank.dim.error) <- c("index", "rank","dim","error")
```

General Tucker decomposition

```{r}
# i is the index of the probability distribution we are going to work with
for (i in 1:20){
  vars <- vars.list[[i]]
  # Create the tensor representing probability distribution of selected variables
  X <- table(d[,vars])
  # X <- table(df.three[,vars])
  X <- X/sum(X)
  X_tensor <- as.tensor(unclass(X))
  
  y <- c()
  for (r in rnks){
    ranks <- rep(r,length(vars))
    result <- tucker(X_tensor, ranks = ranks,max_iter = 100, tol = 1e-06)
    for (j in 1:length(vars)){
      filename <- paste("results/membership-functions-",i,"-", j,"-r-",r,".pdf",sep="")
      pdf(file=filename)
      plot(result$U[[j]][,1],lwd=2,col=col[1],ylim=c(min(result$U[[j]]),max(result$U[[j]])),type="l", xlab="value", 
           ylab="function value", main=paste("Ranks =",r))
      for (k in 2:r){
        lines(result$U[[j]][,k],lwd=2,col=col[k])
      }
      grid()
      dev.off()
    }
    # Reconstruct the tensor
    X_recon <- ttl(result$Z, result$U, ms = 1:length(ranks))
    # normalize so that X_recon is a probability distribution
    X_recon@data <- X_recon@data/sum(X_recon@data)
    # Check the error
    relative_error <- fnorm(X_tensor - X_recon) / fnorm(X_tensor)
    # cat("Relative reconstruction error:", relative_error, "\n")
    y <- c(y, 100*relative_error)
    general.rank.dim.error[(i-1)*length(rnks)+(r-1),] <- c(i, r, X_tensor@num_modes, relative_error*100)
    for (j in 1:length(vars)){
      filename <- paste("results/marg-",i,"-",j,"-r-",r,".pdf",sep="")
      pdf(file=filename)
      plot(apply(X,j,sum),type="l",lwd=2,ylim=c(0,0.5),xlab="value",ylab="probability", main=paste("Ranks =",r))
      lines(apply(X_recon@data,j,sum),lwd=2,col="red")
      legend("topright",col=c("black","red"),lwd=c(2,2), inset=0.02, legend=c("original","approximation"))
      grid()
      dev.off()
    }
  }
}
write.table(general.rank.dim.error, file="results/general-rank-dimensions-errors.csv", sep=",", row.names = FALSE)
```

 ALS Unimodal Tucker decomposition 

```{r}
# i is the index of the probability distribution we are going to work with
for (i in 1:20){
  vars <- vars.list[[i]]
  
  # Create the tensor representing probability distribution of selected variables
  X <- table(d[,vars])
  # X <- table(df.three[,vars])
  X <- X/sum(X)
  X_tensor <- as.tensor(unclass(X))

  for (r in 2:7){
    ranks <- rep(r,length(vars))
    result_als <- tucker_unimodal_als(
      X_tensor,
      ranks = ranks,
      max_iter = 500,
      tol = 1e-4,
      random_init = FALSE,
      verbose = TRUE
    )
    result_normalized <- normalize_factors(result_als)
    # Check reconstruction error of the normalized decomposition
    X_recon <- reconstruct_unimodal(result_normalized)
    X_recon@data <- X_recon@data / sum(X_recon@data)
    relative_error <- fnorm(X_tensor - X_recon) / fnorm(X_tensor)
    cat("Relative reconstruction error:", relative_error, "\n")
    filename <- paste("results/unimodal-membership-functions-",i,"-", j,"-r-",r,".pdf",sep="")
    pdf(file=filename)
      plot_unimodal_results(result_normalized)
    dev.off()
    filename <- paste("results/unimodal-marg-",i,"-",j,"-r-",r,".pdf",sep="")
    pdf(file=filename)
      plot_marginals_comparison(X_tensor, result_normalized, normalize = TRUE)
    dev.off()
    rank.dim.error[(i-1)*length(rnks)+(r-1),] <- c(i, r, X_tensor@num_modes, relative_error*100)
  }
}
write.table(rank.dim.error, file="results/unimodal-dimensions-errors.csv", sep=",", row.names = FALSE)
```

Plot mean relative error of both methods including confidence interval

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Helper function to compute mean, CI lower and upper
summarise_errors <- function(df, method_name) {
  data.frame(
    rank  = as.numeric(names(tapply(df$error, df$rank, mean))),
    mean  = tapply(df$error, df$rank, mean),
    sd    = tapply(df$error, df$rank, sd),
    n     = tapply(df$error, df$rank, length),
    method = method_name
  )
}

df_gen <- as.data.frame(general.rank.dim.error)
df_als <- as.data.frame(rank.dim.error)

gen_summary <- summarise_errors(df_gen, "general")
als_summary <- summarise_errors(df_als, "unimodal")

# Combine and compute 95% CI
combined <- rbind(gen_summary, als_summary)
combined$se    <- combined$sd / sqrt(combined$n)
combined$lower <- combined$mean - 1.96 * combined$se
combined$upper <- combined$mean + 1.96 * combined$se

# Plot
pdf(file="results/unimodal-general-comparison.pdf")
ggplot(combined, aes(x = rank, y = mean, color = method, fill = method)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("unimodal" = "red", "general" = "black")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c("unimodal" = "red", "general" = "black")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(combined$rank)) +
  labs(x = "ranks", y = "Mean Relative Error [%]", title = "") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 22) +  # default is 11, doubling gives 22
  theme(
    plot.title        = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90"),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
    legend.title      = element_blank(),
    legend.position        = "inside",  
    legend.justification.inside = c(1, 1),      # anchor = top-right corner of legend
    legend.position.inside = c(0.98, 0.98),     # top-right corner sits 2% from each margin
    legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", linewidth = 0.3)
  )
dev.off()
```
